denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/13/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103721930          Group #: GRP-1470
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-168411
Claim #: CLM-2026-212911
Date of Service (requested): 04/23/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): [REDACTED]

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 04/25/2026 for the requested service: [REDACTED]
Diagnosis (as submitted): [REDACTED]
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0130 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION

The documentation submitted was reviewed; the clinical notes on file did not support approval.
Our review applied the criteria in the applicable coverage policy and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), including utilization-management provisions. The information on file does not demonstrate that the requested service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The service is denied because plan criteria were not met. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for the benefit category under review. The record did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met.

Additional plan language regarding experimental/investigational definitions, network status, and member cost-sharing may apply and is available upon request.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103721930) and reference PA-2026-168411. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 58-year-old member has Stable angina with positive stress test (I20.9). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Coronary CT angiography recommended as the next appropriate step. Typical angina class II; stress ECG with 2 mm ST depression at 7 METs. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Coronary CT angiography in the setting of Stable angina with positive stress test (I20.9); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
